title: A Formal Business Email
learningGoal: The learner will read a formal Japanese business email to identify Sonkeigo (referring to the client's actions) and Kenjougo (referring to the sender's actions).
instructions: Read this business email from Smith to President Tanaka. Identify which actions belong to the President (Sonkeigo) and which actions belong to Smith (Kenjougo).

contentItems.text: Remember: The person writing the email lowers themselves by using Kenjougo, and elevates the recipient by using Sonkeigo.

readingText:
たなかしゃちょうへ。
ほんじつは ありがとうございました。
しゃちょうが おっしゃいました ことを、わたくしが いたします。
あす、わたくしが そちらに まいります。
よろしく おねがいいたします。
スミスより。
textInTargetLanguage:
たなかしゃちょうへ。
ほんじつは ありがとうございました。
しゃちょうが おっしゃいました ことを、わたくしが いたします。
あす、わたくしが そちらに まいります。
よろしく おねがいいたします。
スミスより。
baseLanguageSupportText: To President Tanaka. Thank you very much for today. I will do the things that the President said. Tomorrow, I will go there (to your company). I humbly look forward to working with you. From Smith.

glossary.targetText: よろしく おねがいいたします
glossary.baseTranslation: I humbly request your favor / Let's work well together

sentenceSupportPairs.targetText: しゃちょうが おっしゃいました ことを、わたくしが いたします。
sentenceSupportPairs.transliteration: Shachou ga osshaimashita koto wo, watakushi ga itashimasu.
sentenceSupportPairs.baseTranslation: I will do the things that the President said.

sentenceSupportPairs.targetText: あす、わたくしが そちらに まいります。
sentenceSupportPairs.transliteration: Asu, watakushi ga sochira ni mairimasu.
sentenceSupportPairs.baseTranslation: Tomorrow, I will go to your company.

comprehensionQuestions.questionText: Who is the sender of this email?
What verb does Smith use to refer to President Tanaka speaking?
What verb does Smith use to state his own intention to do something?
Who is going to the other's company tomorrow?
options: Smith is going to Tanaka's company
Tanaka is going to Smith's company

comprehensionQuestions.questionText: Write the romaji for the humble verb meaning 'I will go'.

correctAnswers.q004: Smith is going to Tanaka's company

expectedSummaryPoints: Smith uses Sonkeigo for Tanaka's actions.
Smith uses Kenjougo for his own actions.

feedbackRules.onPass: Excellent reading! You successfully navigated a formal Japanese business email.
feedbackRules.onFail: Review the verbs. 'Mairimasu' and 'Itashimasu' are humble forms used by the writer (Smith).
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good effort. Notice how the writer uses 'watakushi' instead of 'watashi' to sound even more formal.

audioAssets.audioTranscript: たなかしゃちょうへ。ほんじつは ありがとうございました。しゃちょうが おっしゃいました ことを、わたくしが いたします。あす、わたくしが そちらに まいります。よろしく おねがいいたします。スミスより。

hintText: Look closely at the subject of each sentence. When Tanaka is the subject, Sonkeigo is used. When Smith is the subject, Kenjougo is used.
commonMistakes: Assuming Tanaka is doing the actions in the second half of the email.
